encounter.reason: Second pregnancy confirmation, early pregnancy monitoring, and pregnancy loss (missed abortion)
encounter.location: Rocky Mountain Women's Health, Denver, CO

labs.name: serum beta-hCG (quantitative human chorionic gonadotropin)
labs.interpretation: approximately 5 weeks gestation, within expected range

labs.name: serum beta-hCG (quantitative human chorionic gonadotropin), 48-hour repeat
labs.interpretation: appropriate doubling (doubling time approximately 38 hours, normal)

labs.name: serum beta-hCG (quantitative human chorionic gonadotropin), follow-up

screening.name: PHQ-2 depression screening total score
screening.interpretation: mild, subthreshold; patient endorsed feeling down occasionally; below threshold for positive screen

conditions.name: missed abortion (embryonic demise)
conditions.note: Second consecutive first-trimester pregnancy loss. Embryonic demise confirmed by absent growth and absent cardiac activity on serial transvaginal ultrasounds. Patient chose expectant management; miscarriage completed spontaneously over approximately 10 days.

conditions.name: recurrent pregnancy loss
conditions.note: Two consecutive first-trimester losses. While classically defined as three or more losses, many specialists now initiate evaluation after two consecutive losses, particularly given patient age (35) and emotional distress. Basic RPL workup recommended.

conditions.name: early pregnancy loss (spontaneous abortion), first trimester
conditions.note: Prior loss June 2024. Referenced as context for recurrent pregnancy loss diagnosis. No changes at this encounter.

document_references.description: Documents the second pregnancy confirmation at 5 weeks gestation, serial beta-hCG monitoring showing appropriate initial doubling, 6-week and 7-week transvaginal ultrasound findings confirming embryonic demise, diagnosis of missed abortion managed expectantly, follow-up beta-hCG confirming complete resolution, new diagnosis of recurrent pregnancy loss after two consecutive first-trimester losses, counseling regarding RPL workup rationale, plan for basic RPL panel at follow-up in 6-8 weeks, and emotional support discussion.

diagnostic_reports.description: Intrauterine gestational sac identified with yolk sac present. Fetal pole small for dates. No cardiac activity detected. No adnexal masses or free fluid. Findings concerning for possible early embryonic demise versus early dating; repeat scan recommended in one week.

Fetal pole has not grown since prior scan (2024-10-14). Crown-rump length consistent with 6 weeks 2 days, indicating growth arrest. No cardiac activity detected. Findings consistent with embryonic demise (missed abortion).
